Title: Naringenin confers defence against Phytophthora nicotianae through antimicrobial activity and induction of pathogen resistance in tobacco

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signalling associated with pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced approximately 2-fold after naringenin treatment in HD
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Naringenin induces NtICS1 expression, supporting increased SA biosynthesis during defence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis after naringenin treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtICS1 transcript increased after naringenin treatment. 2. This supports activation of the isochorismate-dependent salicylic acid biosynthesis pathway by naringenin. 3. NtICS1 was used as a defence-response readout rather than genetically manipulated.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker/readout for defence activation by naringenin rather than a directly validated engineering target in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary experimental causal gene for metabolite production; included because it is explicitly assayed as a metabolism-related response gene

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune signalling contributing to pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced about 1.8-fold after naringenin treatment in HD
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Naringenin induces NtEDS1 expression, indicating activation of SA signalling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis after naringenin treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtEDS1 transcript increased following naringenin treatment. 2. This indicates activation of salicylic acid signalling by naringenin. 3. NtEDS1 was monitored as a downstream defence marker and not functionally perturbed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biomarker for naringenin-induced defence signalling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common defence-signalling gene assayed in Results, not as the central manipulated causal gene

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Approximately 1.4-fold higher after naringenin treatment in HD
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Naringenin elevates NtPR1 expression, indicating induced basal resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis after naringenin treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtPR1 expression increased after naringenin treatment. 2. The result supports that naringenin activates basal pathogen resistance. 3. NtPR1 served as a defence output marker in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for defence induction by naringenin
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism gene per se, but directly measured as part of the naringenin defence mechanism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to resistance against Phytophthora nicotianae
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Approximately 3.5-fold higher after naringenin treatment in HD
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Naringenin strongly induces SAR8.2 expression, consistent with enhanced resistance to P. nicotianae
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis after naringenin treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAR8.2 transcript was strongly elevated by naringenin treatment. 2. Because SAR8.2 contributes to resistance to P. nicotianae, its induction supports the defence-promoting action of naringenin. 3. SAR8.2 was used as a downstream resistance marker in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential companion marker/target for improving black shank resistance, though not functionally engineered here
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Resistance role is cited and expression induction is shown here, but direct genetic manipulation was not performed in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial activity against Phytophthora nicotianae; induction of ROS, salicylic acid-associated defence, and pathogen resistance in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots (basal metabolite profiling in uninfected roots); entire plant measured in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora nicotianae infection; exogenous naringenin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/early branch step supplying chalcone precursor to naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with three molecules of malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone, the direct precursor of naringenin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher basal expression in resistant cultivar BH-1 than susceptible XHJ; remained higher after P. nicotianae infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in BH-1 than XHJ without and with P. nicotianae infection
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting/key control step in flavonoid biosynthesis by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes naringenin accumulation and thereby enhances resistance to P. nicotianae; RNAi knockdown lowers naringenin and increases susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference knockdown; RT-qPCR; LC-ESI-MS/MS metabolite quantification; pathogen inoculation phenotyping; pathogen biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-ESI-MS/MS flavonoid metabolomics; targeted LC-ESI-MS/MS naringenin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtCHS expression was higher in resistant BH-1 than in susceptible XHJ. 2. NtCHS-RNAi lines showed strongly reduced NtCHS transcript levels and decreased naringenin content. 3. NtCHS silencing caused greater susceptibility and higher P. nicotianae biomass, linking NtCHS-dependent naringenin biosynthesis to disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CHS controls general flavonoid biosynthesis, so down-regulation likely alters multiple flavonoids beyond naringenin; no negative agronomic effects were reported for exogenous naringenin application, not for NtCHS manipulation itself
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase endogenous naringenin and resistance to tobacco black shank; should be optimized carefully because CHS broadly affects flavonoid-derived traits such as pigmentation and fertility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PAL, C4H, 4CL, and CHI are part of the pathway context, only NtCHS was functionally tested as the core causal biosynthetic gene in Results
